notable_pick_trades: Traded CB Roger McCreary to Rams for conditional 6th-round swap. Acquired 6th from Jets (sent 7th) in Brownlee deal. Traded T'Vondre Sweat to Jets for EDGE Jermaine Johnson (player-for-player, no picks exchanged). Sent 2026 5th to Rams for LB Ernest Jones + 2026 6th.
roster_context: Second straight 3-14 season. New regime: Robert Saleh (HC), Brian Daboll (OC), Gus Bradley (DC). Transitioning from 3-4 to Saleh's 4-3 defense. Building around franchise QB Cam Ward (2025 1st overall) and DT Jeffery Simmons. Massive cap space (~$94M+, most in NFL) but roster is littered with holes. Already cut C Lloyd Cushenberry and S Xavier Woods. Traded T'Vondre Sweat for Jermaine Johnson. Calvin Ridley and L'Jarius Sneed widely expected to be cap casualties. 19 unrestricted free agents including key starters.

needs.explanation: Despite acquiring Jermaine Johnson from the Jets, Tennessee still desperately needs a premier pass rusher. Johnson is coming off an Achilles tear (2024) and had only 3 sacks in 2025 on a one-year $13.4M fifth-year option — he's a reclamation project, not a solution. Outside of Jeffery Simmons, no other pass rusher on the roster has recorded a season with double-digit sacks. 2025 2nd-round pick Femi Oladejo broke his leg in Week 6 and barely contributed. Arden Key and Jihad Ward are UFAs. Saleh's 4-3 system demands dominant edge play, and the draft at #4 offers elite EDGE prospects like Rueben Bain Jr. or Arvell Reese.
needs.depth: Femi Oladejo (2nd-year, returning from broken leg), Arden Key (UFA), Jihad Ward (UFA)

needs.explanation: The cornerback room is arguably the worst in the NFL. L'Jarius Sneed played only 12 games across two injury-plagued seasons and graded at 50.4 PFF in 2025 — his release is a virtual certainty before his $7.5M guarantee date on March 15. Roger McCreary was traded to the Rams midseason. The team fielded 10 different cornerbacks in 2025. Jalyn Armour-Davis and 2025 undrafted rookie Marcus Harris are the only notable returning pieces. Darrell Baker Jr. is an RFA but is a depth piece. Saleh and the Titans have explicitly discussed 'rebuilding the cornerback room' at the Combine.
needs.depth: Marcus Harris (2025 UDFA), Darrell Baker Jr. (RFA) — extremely thin

needs.explanation: Tennessee ranked 27th in PFF receiving grade and 26th in yards per reception in 2025. Calvin Ridley ($26.7M cap hit) is widely expected to be a cap casualty after managing just 17 catches for 303 yards in 7 games before a broken fibula ended his season. Rookies Chimere Dike (bright spot as return specialist/slot) and Elic Ayomanor showed promise but are not proven WR1 options. Ward needs a true No. 1 outside weapon to develop. The team hasn't had a difference-making receiver since trading A.J. Brown in 2022. Van Jefferson is also a UFA.
needs.current_starter: Calvin Ridley (likely cut/restructure), Chimere Dike, Elic Ayomanor
needs.depth: Dike and Ayomanor are promising but unproven sophomores. Van Jefferson UFA.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2, plus free agency (linked to Alec Pierce, Rome Doubs, others)

needs.explanation: Center Lloyd Cushenberry was released with a failed physical designation in February, creating an immediate void. Kevin Zeitler (RG) is a UFA and had an up-and-down 2025 season. Peter Skoronski is elite at LG and entering the final year of his rookie deal (extension candidate). The Titans are actively hunting a starting center in free agency and could also use a developmental guard. Corey Levin played well in relief but is viewed as a backup, not a starter.
needs.current_starter: Peter Skoronski (LG, elite), Center VACANT, RG VACANT (Zeitler UFA)
needs.depth: Corey Levin (UFA, backup-caliber center), Blake Hance (UFA)
needs.draft_range_target: Round 3-4 for developmental IOL, free agency for center

needs.explanation: Jeffery Simmons remains an All-Pro caliber 3-tech, but the Sweat-for-Johnson trade gutted the interior depth. T'Vondre Sweat was PFF's 5th-ranked interior DL and provided elite run-stuffing presence. Sebastian Joseph-Day is a UFA and the Titans let him walk, though they're pursuing John Franklin-Myers as a replacement. Still, they need a nose tackle to replace Sweat's production in Saleh's 4-3 scheme.
needs.depth: Sebastian Joseph-Day (UFA, let walk), no proven NT on roster after Sweat trade
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4, plus free agency (connected to John Franklin-Myers)

needs.explanation: Tony Pollard rushed for 1,082 yards in 2025 but is a cut candidate who would save $7.25M. He turns 29 and is on the wrong side of the RB age cliff. Tyjae Spears provides depth but is not a lead back. The 2026 draft class features potential franchise backs like Jeremiyah Love, who the Titans reportedly circled as a top prospect. Daboll's offense thrives with a dynamic rushing attack.
needs.depth: Tyjae Spears (under contract), thin beyond that
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if top RB like Jeremiyah Love available, otherwise Round 3-4

needs.explanation: Dan Moore Jr. (LT) signed a big contract last offseason and was serviceable but not dominant. JC Latham (RT, 2024 7th overall pick) had a disappointing, injury-plagued sophomore year and was described as 'underwhelming and undisciplined.' Neither tackle needs immediate replacement, but Latham's development is a concern and the team should draft future competition. Oli Udoh (swing tackle) is a UFA.
needs.current_starter: Dan Moore Jr. (LT, under contract), JC Latham (RT, under contract)

needs.explanation: Chig Okonkwo is a UFA and may price himself out of Nashville due to his athleticism and potential. 2025 5th-round pick Gunnar Helm showed flashes as a rookie but is unproven as a starter. Daboll's scheme historically features tight ends heavily (he developed Dawson Knox in Buffalo). If Okonkwo departs, TE becomes a clear need.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 4-5 if Okonkwo re-signed, Round 2-3 if he departs

needs.explanation: Cedric Gray had a solid sophomore year as an off-ball linebacker. Cody Barton is under contract on a 3-year deal. Ernest Jones was acquired from the Rams. This group is adequate but not elite. Saleh's scheme could elevate their play. An upgrade at WILL linebacker would help, but it's not a pressing need compared to other holes.
needs.current_starter: Cedric Gray, Cody Barton, Ernest Jones

needs.explanation: Amani Hooker signed a 3-year, $48.6M extension in September 2025 and is the team's defensive captain. Rookie Kevin Winston Jr. took over starting duties from the now-released Xavier Woods and showed promise. Safety is one of the few secondary positions that isn't a dire need, though adding depth or a versatile nickel safety would help.
needs.current_starter: Amani Hooker (extended through 2028), Kevin Winston Jr.
needs.depth: Thin after top two, but not a critical concern

free_agency_impact: Traded T'Vondre Sweat (NT) to Jets for Jermaine Johnson (EDGE) — partially addresses EDGE but creates IDL hole
Released C Lloyd Cushenberry (failed physical) — creates IOL/center need
Released S Xavier Woods — Kevin Winston Jr. takes over, S not a major need
L'Jarius Sneed (CB) expected to be cut before March 15 guarantee date — massively increases CB need
Calvin Ridley (WR) expected cap casualty ($26.7M hit) — massively increases WR need
Tony Pollard (RB) is a potential cut ($7.25M savings) — could increase RB need
Chig Okonkwo (TE), Kevin Zeitler (IOL), Sebastian Joseph-Day (IDL), Arden Key (EDGE), Jihad Ward (EDGE) all UFAs — multiple position groups lose starters
Titans pursuing John Franklin-Myers (IDL/EDGE FA) to partially replace Sweat/Joseph-Day production
Re-signed S Amani Hooker to 3-year $48.6M extension in September 2025 — safety is stabilized
scheme_fit_notes: Robert Saleh's 4-3 defense (with Gus Bradley as DC) prioritizes winning with the front four. This elevates the need for a true EDGE rusher who can rush off the edge on all three downs. Boundary corners must be physical in press-man and long enough to match up with big WRs. On offense, Brian Daboll's scheme historically features a shifty slot receiver as a safety valve (Cole Beasley role in Buffalo), a vertical outside threat, and heavy tight end usage. A dynamic RB who can catch out of the backfield also fits Daboll's Bills-era scheme. IOL must be able to handle quick passing concepts and zone blocking.
